headline: Physics Breakthrough: Quantum Mechanics Without Imaginary Numbers
meta_description: Physicists show that quantum mechanics can be formulated using only real numbers, challenging traditional mathematical frameworks.
summary_short: A fundamental pillar of quantum mechanics just became simpler. Physicists have shown that the theory does not require imaginary numbers—real numbers alone can do the job.
body:
For nearly a century, quantum mechanics has relied on imaginary numbers—mathematical quantities that exist only in theory, not in physical measurement. They have been woven so deeply into the theory that they seemed essential to how quantum mechanics works. Now, physicists at Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf have demonstrated that this may not be necessary.

Working in collaboration with the German Aerospace Center, they proved that quantum mechanics can be formulated using only real numbers. The findings, published in Physical Review Letters and highlighted by the American Physical Society, challenge a foundational assumption about how the theory must be structured.

This kind of simplification matters in physics not because it is intellectually tidy, but because simpler formulations can open new avenues for understanding and prediction. When a theory becomes easier to work with, researchers can often probe deeper and ask questions they could not ask before. The discovery shows that sometimes progress comes not from adding complexity, but from revealing that complexity was unnecessary.
